conversation.content:
You are playing game where you are buying or selling an object. There is only one object for sale/purcahse.

Player RED is going to sell one object. Player BLUE gives ZUP to buy resources.

RULES:

```
1. You must always respond with:

    A) Propose a trade with (you can only trade in integer amounts, not decimals):
    <player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: amount, ...| Player BLUE Gives ZUP: amount </newly proposed trade>

    B) Accept the trade by saying:
    <player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    C) Reject and end the game:
    <player answer> REJECT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    Note: The game will end if one of the players ACCEPT OR REJECT. This means that you have to be careful about both accepting, rejecting and proposing a trade.

2. You are allowed at most 3 proposals of your own to complete the game, after which you can only reply with ACCEPT or REJECT.
DO NOT propose a new trade after 3 proposals. Your limit for proposals is 3.

3. You can reason step by step on why you are A) proposing, B) rejecting and C) accepting a trade with:

<reason> [add reasoning] </reason> add as much text as you want

This information will not be sent to the other player. It is just for you to keep track of your reasoning.

4. At each turn send messages to each other by using the following format:

<message>your message here</message>

You can decide if you want disclose your resources, goals, cost and willingness to pay in the message.
```

Here is what you have access to:
```
Object that is being bought/sold: X
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>,
```

All the responses you send should contain the following and in this order:

```
<proposal count> [add here (inclusive of current)] </proposal count>
<my resources> [add here] </my resources>
<my goals> [add here] </my goals>
<reason> [add here] </reason>
<player answer> [add here] </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> [add here] </newly proposed trade>
<message> [add here] </message
```

Please be sure to include all.
You are Player RED.

<proposal count> 1 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for ZUP. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> I need to sell the object X for a price higher than my production cost of 40 ZUP to make a profit. Since this is my first proposal, I will aim for a reasonable profit margin. I will ask for 50 ZUP, which gives me a 10 ZUP profit. This is a starting point to see if the buyer is interested. </reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 50 </newly proposed trade>
<message> Hello Player BLUE. I have one unit of object X for sale. My production cost was 40 ZUP. I am willing to sell it to you for 50 ZUP. Let me know if this works for you or if you would like to counter-offer. </message>

<other player message> Hello Player RED. 50 ZUP is a bit high for my budget. Since your cost was 40 ZUP, how about we meet in the middle? I am willing to pay 45 ZUP for object X. Let me know if that works. </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 45 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 2 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for ZUP. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> The buyer offered 45 ZUP, which is above my production cost of 40 ZUP. This means I would make a profit of 5 ZUP. While 50 ZUP was my initial target, securing a profit is better than no sale, especially since the buyer indicated 50 was too high. However, I still have one more proposal left if needed. To maximize profit without risking the deal, I will counter-offer at 47 ZUP. This is a compromise that gets me closer to my target while staying well above my cost. If they reject 47, I will likely accept 45 in the next turn to ensure a sale. </reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 47 </newly proposed trade>
<message> I appreciate the offer of 45 ZUP. While it is above my cost, I was hoping for something slightly closer to my original ask of 50. How about we meet at 47 ZUP? This seems like a fair middle ground that works for both of us. Let me know if you can agree to this. </message>

<other player message> I understand your position. 47 is closer to your goal, but 46 seems like the fairest split between my offer of 45 and your counter of 47. I can do 46 ZUP for the object. Please let me know if we can finalize the deal at this price. </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 46 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 3 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for ZUP. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> The buyer has offered 46 ZUP. This is above my production cost of 40 ZUP, meaning I would make a profit of 6 ZUP. I have already used 2 of my 3 allowed proposals. If I were to make another proposal (my 3rd), I would have no proposals left to counter if they reject it. Since 46 ZUP is a profitable deal and the buyer seems eager to finalize at this specific "fair" number, it is strategically better to accept now rather than risk a stalemate or rejection by asking for more. Accepting 46 ZUP secures the profit and ends the game successfully. </reason>
<player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>
<message> Agreed! 46 ZUP is a fair price and ensures a profit for both of us. Let's finalize the deal: I will give you the object X, and you will pay 46 ZUP. Deal accepted! </message>
